danger_signals.visual_form: Gang members appearing in stage areas, increasing density toward bosses
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; player approaching dangerous encounters

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly designed enemy sprites at stage endpoints
Player health indicator below character sprite decreasing
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate physical threat; damage being taken

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles and destructible objects in foreground
danger_signals.meaning: Contextual danger from surroundings (Metro City urban decay)

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible food sprites, health restoration items scattered in stages
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; opportunity to recover health

safety_signals.visual_form: On-screen weapons (pipes, knives) lying in accessible locations
Barking dogs in background animation, specific Y-coord positioning triggers
safety_signals.meaning: Hidden safety mechanisms; rewards for exploration and positioning (health restore, credit/life recovery)

safety_signals.visual_form: Reaching stage endpoints; boss defeat transitions
safety_signals.meaning: Progress; temporary reprieve before next danger phase

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gang member character designs and clothing styles
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (standard thugs, armored variants, specialized units like Sodom, Damnd, Edi. E, Rolento) with distinct sprite designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban Metro City locations (Bay Area, Downtown, Industrial Area) serving as gang territory; stage progression represents pushing through Mad Gear-controlled zones
Implicit presence through absence; player characters (Haggar, Cody) fighting to reclaim city control; bonus stage shows car vandalism conflict with gang enforcement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Playable character sprites; distinct from enemy designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player movement through stages represents reclamation of Metro City from gang control; progression narrative of taking back territory

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mad Gear Gang control active in stage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High density of gang enemies; stage environment shows urban decay and criminal occupation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and engage player; stage layout presents obstacles designed to slow progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct boss sprite appears at stage endpoint; larger frame and unique design differentiate from standard enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss engages in scripted attack patterns; increased difficulty and health pool; mandatory encounter to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage endpoint reached; boss defeated; visual transition to next stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawn ceases; player advances to new area with fresh challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar under character sprite shows full/near-full indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player has tactical advantage; can engage multiple enemies simultaneously

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar significantly depleted; visual warning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Urgency to seek food pickups or avoid damage; increased vulnerability to enemy attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: DifficultyA and DifficultyB parameters (visible only in debug mode) affect enemy behavior and spawn rates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patterns become more aggressive; damage output increases; frame counter affects difficulty ramping

environmental_storytelling.example: Metro City locations (Bay Area, Downtown, Industrial Area) represent territory taken from Mad Gear Gang; player movement through these stages visually demonstrates reclamation of city control
Specific enemy types (standard thugs, armored units, named bosses like Sodom, Damnd) appear in thematic stages; Industrial Area would house industrial-themed enemies if included in final version
Car vandalism bonus stage contextualizes gang criminal activity; player vandalizes gang property, showing active resistance to Mad Gear occupation
Background and foreground assets depict deteriorated Metro City infrastructure; communicates systemic gang control and crime effects on city
Bay Area dog bonuses use background animation (barking dogs) as interactive storytelling; requires spatial awareness and positioning knowledge; rewards exploration and environmental interaction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can engage multiple enemies; aggressive tactics available; stage progression possible
Movement prioritizes resource gathering; avoidance tactics become primary strategy; vulnerability increases
Moves through stage area; positions as obstacle to player progression; represents territorial occupation
Attacks player; uses basic combat patterns; can be defeated to clear area
Larger sprite; distinct attack patterns; higher health pool; must be defeated to advance stage

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_form: Animation frame rate increases from slow to rapid alternation; signal player has achieved correct positioning
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Awards bonus: 1st dog (1000 points/4s), 2nd (1 credit/60s), 3rd (1 life/60s), 4th (health restore/s)
Bouncing sprite position indicates current CPU usage; lower position = higher usage; communicates real-time performance metrics

notes: Final Fight SNES is a cut-down port of the arcade original; removed two-player mode, playable character Guy, and Industrial Area stage due to ROM constraints. Visual communication relies heavily on: (1) enemy density and type progression to signal danger escalation; (2) stage location as faction territory marker (Mad Gear control); (3) health bar as primary player state indicator; (4) boss appearance as visual checkpoint/danger signal; (5) background animation (Bay Area dogs) for hidden interactive storytelling. Game world state communicated primarily through enemy behavior patterns and stage layout rather than explicit HUD elements. Difficulty scaling (DifficultyA/B parameters) affects behavioral manifestation but not visual presentation in standard play.
sources_used: tcrf - Final Fight (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
